rxn_class_summary: Electrophotocatalytic trifluoromethylative (or difluoromethylative) radical cyclization of unactivated alkenes using CF3SO2Na and eosin Y under combined visible light and electrochemistry to access fluoromethylated N-heterocycles.
electrochemical_role: Anode reoxidizes the reduced eosin Y radical anion (PC•−) back to ground-state PC, closing a reductive quenching photocatalytic cycle; cathode reduces protons to H2 (no external oxidant needed).

key_intermediates.name: Excited-state eosin Y (PC*), E_red* = 1.51 V vs Ag/AgCl
CF3 radical generated by SET oxidation of CF3SO2Na and loss of SO2
Alkyl radical A from CF3• addition to the 2-methylallyl alkene of 1a
Cyclohexadienyl radical B from intramolecular radical addition onto the 2-phenyl ring
Cationic Wheland-type intermediate C after SET oxidation of B by PC*

electron_flow: Visible light excites eosin Y to PC*, which oxidizes CF3SO2Na (E_ox = 0.89 V) by SET to give SO2 and CF3•; CF3• adds to the alkene to form alkyl radical A that cyclizes onto the pendant aryl to form cyclohexadienyl radical B, which is oxidized by a second PC* (or PC*) to cation C; deprotonation rearomatizes to product 3a, while PC•− is reoxidized to PC at the anode and protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully suppressed (0% yield of 3a), consistent with radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Trace 3a; DPE–CF3 adduct 18 isolated in 36% yield, directly trapping CF3•.

probe_reagents.name: CuCl2
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3a, consistent with single-electron transfer step.

probe_reagents.name: light on/off and current on/off
probe_reagents.observation: Product formation halts when either light or current is removed; no extended radical chain; under dark periods reaction slows but does not fully stop due to accumulated oxidizing species.

notes: Authors explicitly exclude an electron-primed photocatalytic mechanism and an oxidative-quenching photoredox cycle because neither ground-state nor photoexcited eosin Y is preferentially oxidized at the anode under the reaction conditions (no new oxidation wave under blue light in CV). Stern–Volmer quenching of EY* by CF3SO2Na and a bathochromic shift in UV–vis upon mixing EY with 2a suggest a non-bonding interaction and SET from 2a to PC*; H2 evolution at the cathode was confirmed by gas detection. A sunlight-driven gram-scale variant also works (86% yield), supporting practicality.
